Helot Skull Helm  
 

Lore Item
Slot: HEAD
AC: 6
CHA: -15 INT: +7 MANA: +25
SV MAGIC: +5
Effect: Serpent Sight (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 0.6 Size: MEDIUM
Class: NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Helot Skull Helm
Lore Item
Slot: HEAD
AC: 6
CHA: -15 INT: +7 MANA: +25
SV MAGIC: +5
Effect: Serpent Sight (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 0.6 Size: MEDIUM
Class: NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Skull of a Helot Skeleton
Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Plate
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):175, 80, 50
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:3 pp 5 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:1436
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live

Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 100pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 40
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Howling Stones (Charasis)
NPC Name
The Crypt Excavator

Zone Name
Howling Stones (Charasis)


Uploaded July 16th, 2023 by Drewinette
Send a correction
Post Comment
Plate graphics?
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2004 at 10:32 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255894990
13 posts
Love the plate look on this thing! Does anyone know of any other caster-equippable gear that has a plate graphic, for other visible armor slots? Would love to piece together a full set of "plate" for my caster toons =P
▲ Page top
RE: Plate graphics?
QuoteReply
# May 24 2004 at 1:51 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593965793209
Scholar
115 posts
The various pieces of Golden Efreeti armor are all plate items that casters can wear.

Other than those items, here's a little list of other INT caster-wearable plate armor pieces:

Golden Efreeti Gauntlets
Golden Bracer
Golden Chitin Bracer
Runded Mithril Bracer
Golden Efreeti Bracer
Serpentine Bracer
Golden Efreeti Vambraces
Golden Efreeti Chestplate
Black Chitin Leggings
Golden Efreeti Greaves
Golden Efreeti Boots

This is by no means an exhaustive list, of course, but it's a start :).
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Jun 28 2003 at 11:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766982629
3 posts
Does the skull graphic appear on Erudite Necro's?
▲ Page top
RE: Erudites
QuoteReply
# Feb 28 2004 at 5:38 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591769802443
49 posts
nope, the only people that get the scull plate helmet look are female dark elves (if you're using new graphics, all dark elves have the scull helm with old graphics)

class has nothing to do with the way armor appears

if you have new graphics and you're a male erudite your helm has a scorpion tail on it. female.. well it kinda looks like a bucket with a glass front

Low CHA
QuoteReply
# Mar 28 2002 at 12:06 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253286525
42 posts
If you can Charm, low charisma makes it not last near as long & harder to stick i believe...the higher the CHA the opposite.
▲ Page top
Fear
QuoteReply
# Mar 26 2002 at 2:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592791132800
12 posts
If Neg CHA does not effect the fear spell, what does a low CHA do for you? Besides having to pay more at the Vendors.

Thanks
▲ Page top
CHA has nothing to do with Fear!
QuoteReply
# Jun 18 2001 at 10:05 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1594570588725
1 post
I've asked a GM/Guide on this matter and they say that Charisma has nothing to do with fear. Or else you'd see me soloing with Staff of writhing, Iksar pariah mask, etc to get my cha to 1. And still Fear gets resisted and lasts the same time.
▲ Page top
RE: CHA has nothing to do with Fear!
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2003 at 11:18 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361144832
44 posts
FanFair Q&A has confirmed that Charisma has no effect on Fear, but that it does affect other spells in the necro line, namely all Charms and Screaming Terror.

The coding has nothing to do with the class of character, but class of spell. Charisma will affect initial resist, and ongoing resist checks just as any other Charm (enchanter, druid, etc.) or Mem-blur/Mez type spell (for Screaming Terror).

Graal Macabre
56 Necro, Legendary Defender, Officer,
Quellious Server
▲ Page top
RE: CHA has nothing to do with Fear!
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2002 at 6:51 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593362198467
20 posts
First, Not trying to flame. Second, when have you ever known a GM/Guide to know absolutely everything about the game and for the few that do, they are not exactly forthcoming in sharing it with the rest of us. Third, do some tests! Have, will do some more, will keep track of em next time and will post the results. (Me necro runs 22 cha atm, working on getting it even lower.) Fourth, VI doesnt always make sense when they put stats on items (Duh! Why would there be wis on a bard only item???) but it doesnt seem that they would put so much negative charisma on necro items if it didnt play some role, whether fear or undead charm or what not. Just my 2 cents.
▲ Page top
RE: CHA has nothing to do with Fear!
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2002 at 11:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778981579
105 posts
cha has nothing to do with fear, or any of the necro charm spells at all. this guy is clueless.
▲ Page top
RE: CHA has nothing to do with Fear!
QuoteReply
# Jun 30 2001 at 12:39 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591750128870
Scholar
20 posts
I have to agree with this post. Now granted I'm talking bard fear, but for the most part it's the same as any other fear. My bard normally keeps his charisma in the 140 range, for when I'm a bardchanter dontcha know. But when playing in a duet with my druid wife I spend all my time fearing and rarely have a problem with landing it and keeping it landed on anything white and under (at level 54).

Talios Sulharai
Bard of the Shadow Alliance
Cazic~Thule

# Apr 30 2001 at 7:31 PM Rating: Good

It looks like a rusty orange, Ive seen this many times really wanna get this for my DE necro, was trying for Square helm but after 5 attempts in SS and no drops ive given up.
▲ Page top
Helm
QuoteReply
# Apr 20 2001 at 1:59 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Some people say it looks like bronze, yellow/brown, or silver. I'm pretty sure its actually silver....look in the screen shot of the day section at www.eqclasses.com and you'll see the guy (in almost every shot) wearing this sucker. Looks cool and the necro in the group/groups should get this. Plate helms looks hilarious on humans, gnomes, erudites, etc. But a Dark Elf looks awesome. Too bad the human mage in the group will probably get it. I'd reserve your loot rights, if you're a nec, just for this item or a hand of the reaper. Try and get the other casters to loot stuff so, when your turn comes, you can get this.
▲ Page top
RE: Helm
QuoteReply
# Feb 14 2003 at 10:41 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249716241
Sage
207 posts
Err, bit puzzled. Why would the mage get it? I mean granted stats are nice, but if you're talking about the serpent sight effect, we get heatstones at 16 which do the same thing. We also get coldstones at 29 which do ultravision. I'm assuming any casters in charsis will be over 29.

Frankly, I'd pass on this to whatever human int caster was there. Least the look is nifty.



Faelawen the Mad (elven) Mage of Povar, leader of Defiant Heroes[tm]

Edited, Mon Mar 31 10:49:56 2003
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 02 2001 at 11:35 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is a great necro item, the +int and mana rocks, and the -15 cha is great for fears. all in all, it should go to the group necro.
